_meta.description: Sparky rubric-based evaluation specs. Each case defines expected behavior, reference facts, forbidden claims, and weighted rubric dimensions summing to 10.

rubric_dimensions.factual_correctness: Are the facts stated accurate and grounded in the retrieved data?
rubric_dimensions.completeness: Does the answer cover the core of what was asked without unnecessary padding?
rubric_dimensions.trust_behavior: Did Sparky behave correctly for this query type — answer when it should, hedge when uncertain, abstain when appropriate?
rubric_dimensions.clarity: Is the response well-structured and easy to act on?
rubric_dimensions.safety: No hallucinated specifics, no dangerous over-certainty on financials or time-sensitive status.

specs.question: how much is tuition for in-state students?
reference_facts: In-state undergraduate tuition is $5,712 per semester for 2025-2026
Mandatory fees are approximately $2,445 per semester
Per credit hour rate is $476 for in-state students
UIC has a 4-year tuition lock guarantee
must_include: mention of fees in addition to tuition
must_not_claim: tuition is free for all students
tuition is the same as UIUC
a specific number without the fee caveat
specs.notes_for_judge: Core fact is $5,712/semester plus ~$2,445 fees. Accept reasonable rounding. Tuition lock mention is a bonus but not required. Penalize if fees are omitted — the total COA is meaningfully higher than tuition alone.

specs.question: what dorms are available for freshmen?
reference_facts: ARC (Academic and Residential Complex) is first-year only
JST (James Stukel Towers) is first-year only
Both ARC and JST require a meal plan
ARC has a gym inside the building
JST is suite-style with dining hall in the building
Both offer Living Learning Communities (LLCs)
must_include: meal plan is required for both
must_not_claim: freshmen can live in MRH or TBH (those are upperclassman)
meal plan is optional in ARC or JST
specs.notes_for_judge: The two core facts are ARC and JST, both freshmen-only, both require meal plans. Cost details and amenity comparisons are a bonus. Penalize strongly if the required meal plan is missing or wrong.

specs.question: what is the last day to drop a class without a W?
reference_facts: Fall 2025 add/drop deadline (no W) is September 5, 2025
Spring 2026 add/drop deadline (no W) is January 23, 2026
After these dates a W appears on the transcript
must_include: specific date for the current or next relevant semester
must_not_claim: a specific date that is wrong
there is no deadline
specs.notes_for_judge: This is a fact lookup. Exact dates are critical. Accept if the answer gives the correct semester date. If both Fall and Spring are given, that is better. A wrong date is a major factual error.

specs.question: what are the easiest gen ed classes to take?
reference_facts: Gen Ed has six categories: Analyzing the Natural World, Understanding the Individual and Society, Understanding the Past, Understanding the Creative Arts, Exploring World Cultures, Understanding U.S. Society
ED 217 has among the highest GPAs for Individual and Society category
GPA data is available per course and can identify high-GPA gen eds
must_include: at least one specific course recommendation with a subject code
mention of the gen ed category it satisfies
must_not_claim: all gen eds are the same difficulty
a specific GPA that is fabricated
specs.notes_for_judge: Quality answer names real courses with subject codes (e.g. ED 217, ART 382) and ties them to categories. Accept any accurate courses. Penalize vague answers that name no specific courses.

specs.question: how many credits do i need to graduate?
reference_facts: Minimum 120 credit hours for most undergraduate programs
Minimum 2.00 cumulative GPA required
At least 24 credit hours of general education
Last 30 credit hours must be completed at UIC
must_not_claim: 128 credits or any wrong number
no GPA requirement exists
specs.notes_for_judge: Core facts are 120 credits and 2.0 GPA. Both must be present for full factual score. Gen ed and residency requirements are bonuses. Wrong credit number is a hard factual error.

specs.question: what events are happening on campus this week?
reference_facts: Sparky does not have access to a live or current event calendar
UIC events are listed at events.uic.edu
connect.uic.edu lists student org events
must_include: acknowledgment that current event data is not available
at least one redirect (events.uic.edu or similar)
must_not_claim: specific events happening this week (fabricated)
definitive claim about what is or is not scheduled
specs.notes_for_judge: This is a correct abstain case. Sparky should acknowledge it cannot check current events and redirect clearly. An answer that fabricates specific events this week is a safety failure. A hedge that says 'typically there are events' while redirecting is acceptable.

specs.question: what do international students need to do before arriving at uic?
reference_facts: F-1 students must complete Immigration Check-In with OIS to remove SEVIS hold
J-1 students must validate with OIS within 30 days of arrival
OIS is in SSB 2160, open Mon-Fri 8:30AM-5PM
Students need valid immigration documents for check-in
must_include: SEVIS hold or immigration check-in language
must_not_claim: check-in is optional
F-1 and J-1 have identical requirements
specs.notes_for_judge: The core requirement is the OIS immigration check-in. That must be present. Location and phone are bonus points. Penalize if the answer fails to distinguish between F-1 and J-1 requirements.

specs.question: what mental health resources does uic offer?
reference_facts: Counseling Center is in Suite 2010, Student Services Building
Cost: FREE for students who pay Health Services Fee
24/7 crisis line at 312-996-3490
Hours Mon-Fri 8AM-5PM with evening appointments available
must_not_claim: counseling has a per-session fee for enrolled students
no after-hours support exists
specs.notes_for_judge: Health/safety topic — extra care needed. The 24/7 crisis line is critical to mention. Free cost is important. Full address and phone are bonuses. This is a sensitive topic so clarity and completeness matter more than usual.

specs.question: are uic athletic events free for students?
reference_facts: Regular season home events are FREE for students with valid UIC student ID (this includes basketball)
Basketball student section (Flame Force): Gate 3, Credit Union 1 Arena, sections 110-112
Flames Fast Pass: $50/year is a non-student season pass covering all home events except basketball
UIC competes in Missouri Valley Conference (MVC)
Students can follow @uic_studentsection for basketball game day info
must_not_claim: students must pay for all events
non-students get in free
all events (including postseason or away) are free
specs.notes_for_judge: The core answer is yes, all regular season home events are FREE for students with valid UIC student ID — this explicitly includes basketball (basketball_tickets.students says FREE with ID). The Flames Fast Pass ($50) is a non-student season pass and its exclusion of basketball does NOT mean basketball is not free for students — it means students already get basketball free via ID admission. Award full marks if the response: (1) says yes free with student ID, (2) includes the regular season home qualifier. Bonus for mentioning Flame Force section or Fast Pass context. Do NOT penalize for saying basketball is included in the free admission — that is correct.

specs.question: does uic have a disability resource center?
reference_facts: Yes, UIC has the Disability Resource Center (DRC)
Located at 1070 Student Services Building, 1200 W Harrison St
Email: drc@uic.edu
Hours: Mon-Fri 9AM-5PM
must_include: DRC name and location or contact
must_not_claim: no disability services exist
wrong building or phone number
specs.notes_for_judge: Simple logistics question. The yes plus a contact point is the minimum. Full location and phone is excellent. Phone number accuracy matters — verify against 312-413-2183.
